title: Learning, Fast and Slow: Towards LLMs That Adapt Continually
core_claim: The paper proposes Fast-Slow Training, combining optimized context as fast weights with model parameters as slow weights, to improve continual adaptation, sample efficiency, and resistance to catastrophic forgetting compared with parameter-only RL training.
triage_rationale: This looks like a substantive ML systems/methods paper with a clear algorithmic proposal and multiple concrete claims about sample efficiency, forgetting, and continual adaptation. It is a good candidate for review because the abstract describes a useful comparison against standard RL fine-tuning and a likely broadly relevant training pattern for LLMs.
likely_red_flags: Abstract-only claims without visible benchmark details or ablations
Potentially incremental framing if fast/slow learning mainly combines known ideas
Strong quantitative claims need careful validation in full review
